text: The surgeon makes an incision at the three o'clock position from the superior part of the dentate line to the skin. Dissection begins at the dentate line and continues around the anal canal using electrocautery and curved scissors, separating the anal mucosa from the underlying anal sphincter muscle.

The surgeon applies the same dissection technique over the nine o'clock position, fully separating the remaining anal mucosa flap from the underlying anal sphincter muscle using electrocautery and curved scissors.
The surgeon excises the hemorrhoid tissue from the mucosa flap, estimating the appropriate length of the mucosa flap to reach the neodental line.
The surgeon uses a marking pen to outline the excision line on the anal mucosa. The redundant mucosal flap is then trimmed to the marked line.

The surgeon sutures the edge of the mucosal flap through the sphincter muscle from the midline, re-approximating it to the rectal mucosa using interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures for neodental line reconstruction. The large hemorrhoid tissue is visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon closes the anal skin using Kega sutures, completing the neodentaline reconstruction procedure.
